text.title: Contextual Inquiry Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the contextual-inquiry methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Researcher MUST adopt master-apprentice stance: user explains the work; researcher questions to understand, never to advise. Suggestion-giving during the session is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Suggesting solutions contaminates observation; master-apprentice preserves the user's actual workflow.

rule.statement: Session MUST happen in the user's environment (office, factory floor, hospital, home) — not a lab.
rule.rationale: Environment shapes workflow; lab settings strip context that produces breakdowns and workarounds.

rule.statement: Notes MUST capture verbatim quotes tied to participant ID + timestamp; paraphrased summaries alone are insufficient.
rule.rationale: Verbatim quotes ground later affinity diagrams + persona work; paraphrases lose nuance and inject researcher bias.

rule.statement: Interpretation session MUST run within 48 hours of the field session with >=2 researchers.
rule.rationale: Memory decays fast; same-week interpretation captures non-verbal cues + context invisible in raw notes.

rule.statement: Written informed consent MUST cover observation, recording, photo/screen capture, anonymisation, retention period; no session without signed consent.
rule.rationale: Legal + ethical baseline; missing consent invalidates the data and exposes the org.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
